jokes.text: My thing, not a rape.
jokes.setup: Mac summarizing his 'sin' from the previous episode
jokes.punchline: Casually clarifying something wasn't rape as if that's reassuring
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark comedy economy - three words that reveal character's complete lack of self-awareness about how disturbing this clarification is

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: My thing, not a rape.

jokes.text: Charlie's escalating panic about leaving Philadelphia leading to being 'stuck in a box on a sinking ship'
jokes.setup: Charlie explaining his regret about leaving Philly
jokes.punchline: The progression from never leaving to being trapped in the worst possible scenario
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific paranoia escalation that feels authentic to Charlie's anxious personality

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Charlie's escalating panic about leaving Philadelphia leading to being 'stuck in a box on a sinking ship'

jokes.text: Life is just a series of events with no meaning or plan... we're tops, we're bottoms.
jokes.setup: Mac giving an existential speech about the meaninglessness of life
jokes.punchline: Ending with gay sexual positions as part of his philosophical observations
jokes.explanation: Unexpected juxtaposition of pseudo-philosophical rambling with specific sexual references reveals character's preoccupations

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Life is just a series of events with no meaning or plan... we're tops, we're bottoms.

jokes.text: Extended argument about what sound boat engines make with increasingly elaborate sound effects
jokes.setup: Characters debating whether they can hear the ship's engine
jokes.punchline: Devolving into ridiculous vocal sound effects and pedantic arguments
jokes.explanation: Classic Sunny extended argument about something meaningless, though it runs a bit long

jokes.text: I carry a little piece of onion around with me to elicit tears... in case someone needs me to feel something
jokes.setup: Dennis making an analogy about fake engine sounds
jokes.punchline: Revealing he literally carries onions to fake emotional responses
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis character moment revealing his sociopathic manipulation tactics as casual conversation

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I carry a little piece of onion around with me to elicit tears... in case someone needs me to feel something

jokes.text: No, I'm taking a vacation from myself.
jokes.setup: Asked if he has an onion in his pocket right now
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning he's on vacation from his manipulative behavior
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly casual delivery of the idea that emotional manipulation is work he takes breaks from

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: No, I'm taking a vacation from myself.

jokes.text: Four hours, six minutes.
jokes.setup: Asked how long they've been talking about boat engine sounds
jokes.punchline: Precise timing of their ridiculous argument
jokes.explanation: The precision of the timing makes their waste of time even more absurd

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Four hours, six minutes.

jokes.text: Pro: We've cut our conflict resolution time in half. Four hours, that's good for us.
jokes.setup: Mac finding positive aspects of their situation
jokes.punchline: Being proud that four hours of arguing is an improvement for them
jokes.explanation: Perfect group dynamic joke - they're so dysfunctional that massive time waste is progress

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Pro: We've cut our conflict resolution time in half. Four hours, that's good for us.

jokes.text: Oi, oi, oi, oi, oi, oi, oi, oi, oi, oi! / You sound like a soccer hooligan.
jokes.setup: Mac using some technique to calm down Charlie
jokes.punchline: Dennis pointing out he sounds like a soccer hooligan
jokes.explanation: Simple observational humor about Mac's bizarre calming technique

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Oi, oi, oi, oi, oi, oi, oi, oi, oi, oi!

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: You sound like a soccer hooligan.

jokes.text: When my dad was in solitary confinement, I used to write to him... He never wrote back, but if he did, he would've said, 'Son, you've got to keep your mind active. Also, I love you.'
jokes.setup: Mac sharing advice about staying mentally active
jokes.punchline: Admitting his dad never wrote back but fantasizing about what he would have said
jokes.explanation: Sad-funny moment revealing Mac's desperate need for paternal approval

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: When my dad was in solitary confinement, I used to write to him... He never wrote back, but if he did, he would've said, 'Son, you've got to keep your mind active. Also, I love you.'

jokes.text: Remind me who you are again?
jokes.setup: Frank suggesting what Mac's dad might say instead
jokes.punchline: Implying his own father wouldn't remember him
jokes.explanation: Brutal callback that undercuts Mac's fantasy with harsh reality

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Remind me who you are again?

jokes.text: I can't do Obama 'cause he's a black man and I'm a white woman. Can you give me a white woman?
jokes.setup: Dee being asked to do impressions and refusing Obama
jokes.punchline: Her logic about racial/gender matching for impressions
jokes.explanation: Sets up for bigger payoff but works as character moment showing Dee's misguided principles

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: I can't do Obama 'cause he's a black man and I'm a white woman. Can you give me a white woman?

jokes.text: C.C.H. Pounder... Goddamn it, Dutch! What other errands do you have us running for the D.A.?
jokes.setup: Dennis suggesting an obscure black actress for Dee to impersonate
jokes.punchline: Dee doing a surprisingly accurate impression
jokes.explanation: Subverts expectation that Dee would be bad at impressions, though somewhat niche reference

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: C.C.H. Pounder... Goddamn it, Dutch! What other errands do you have us running for the D.A.?

jokes.text: I just assumed she's a no-nonsense black broad from the precinct. That's exactly who she is.
jokes.setup: Frank admitting he doesn't know who C.C.H. Pounder is
jokes.punchline: His racist assumption happening to be accurate
jokes.explanation: Frank's casual racism accidentally being right is darkly funny but relies on stereotypes

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: I just assumed she's a no-nonsense black broad from the precinct. That's exactly who she is.

jokes.text: Hey, yo, what up, son? What's up wit' Congress bein' all up in my ass and shit, brother?
jokes.setup: Dee attempting to do Obama impression
jokes.punchline: Horrifically racist interpretation using street slang
jokes.explanation: Cringe works for character (Dee being obliviously offensive) but the joke itself is just shock value racism

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Hey, yo, what up, son? What's up wit' Congress bein' all up in my ass and shit, brother?

jokes.text: Hey, yo, where my senators at, dawg?
jokes.setup: Dee doubling down on her terrible Obama impression
jokes.punchline: Making it even more racist and inappropriate
jokes.explanation: Escalation of cringe but the joke is just 'racism is bad' without much craft beyond that

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Hey, yo, where my senators at, dawg?

jokes.text: Can I have a broad with me with big tits? / No, because that's gonna force us to imagine things that we don't want to imagine.
jokes.setup: Frank wanting to add elements to their imaginary dinner party
jokes.punchline: Mac's logical objection to Frank's perverted addition
jokes.explanation: Good character interplay - Frank being creepy, Mac being weirdly logical about it

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Can I have a broad with me with big tits?

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: No, because that's gonna force us to imagine things that we don't want to imagine.

jokes.text: You're gonna start with, like, a big hunk of chicken. You know, like, boiled and skinless... And then, um, you're gonna want brown with the white, right? So, like, maybe, like, um, some, like, brown rice. No... no butter or-or seasoning
jokes.setup: Mac describing his imaginary dinner party menu
jokes.punchline: Describing the most unappetizing, health-obsessed meal possible
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mac character moment - his fitness obsession making even fantasy food terrible

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: You're gonna start with, like, a big hunk of chicken. You know, like, boiled and skinless... And then, um, you're gonna want brown with the white, right? So, like, maybe, like, um, some, like, brown rice. No... no butter or-or seasoning

jokes.text: Blue is not a flavor. / How am I gonna taste blue? / Well, just imagine what blue tastes like. / It does... It just tastes like blue.
jokes.setup: Mac offering blue Gatorade in his imaginary meal
jokes.punchline: His circular logic about blue being a flavor
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist logic chain about synesthesia and Mac's confident stupidity

dialogue.line: Blue is not a flavor.
How am I gonna taste blue?
Well, just imagine what blue tastes like.
It does... It just tastes like blue.

jokes.text: Mine's kind of tough. D-Did you overcook this? / No, I didn't cook it at all. / Spit it out! It's raw! You're gonna get sick!
jokes.setup: Characters eating the imaginary food
jokes.punchline: Frank complaining about raw chicken in the completely imaginary meal
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation of absurdity - they're taking the fake meal so seriously they're having food safety concerns

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Mine's kind of tough. D-Did you overcook this?

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: No, I didn't cook it at all.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Spit it out! It's raw! You're gonna get sick!

jokes.text: I'm not imagining your meal. No, I'm imagining a lovely coq au vin. Braised with lardons and mushrooms.
jokes.setup: Dennis being asked to participate in Mac's terrible imaginary meal
jokes.punchline: Revealing he's been imagining an elegant French dish instead
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis character moment - his narcissism extends to hijacking even imaginary experiences for something more sophisticated

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I'm not imagining your meal. No, I'm imagining a lovely coq au vin. Braised with lardons and mushrooms.

jokes.text: She got her foot in my raw chicken! Get your hooker's foot out of my chicken, man!
jokes.setup: Frank's imaginary date getting involved in the meal chaos
jokes.punchline: Charlie treating contamination of imaginary food as a real crisis
jokes.explanation: Continues the escalation of taking imaginary things seriously, with good visual absurdity

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: She got her foot in my raw chicken! Get your hooker's foot out of my chicken, man!

jokes.text: I'm flipping the table! Smash! Crash! Oh! Now I'm breaking a plate! Boom! Smash!
jokes.setup: Mac getting frustrated with his imaginary dinner party falling apart
jokes.punchline: Violently destroying imaginary furniture with sound effects
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy escalation that feels earned from the building frustration, though not particularly original

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: I'm flipping the table! Smash! Crash! Oh! Now I'm breaking a plate! Boom! Smash!

jokes.text: Extended awkward silence broken only by rhythmic tapping
jokes.setup: Group trying to sit in silence to pass time
jokes.punchline: Someone starting to make rhythmic noises, ruining the quiet
jokes.explanation: Simple visual gag about their inability to stay quiet, but not particularly memorable

jokes.text: I hated that show, Stomp. / Ugh, I hate rhythm.
jokes.setup: Dennis commenting on Dee's rhythmic tapping
jokes.punchline: Frank's even more extreme position against rhythm itself
jokes.explanation: Good character moments showing their pretentious/cranky attitudes, with Frank's line being absurdly extreme

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I hated that show, Stomp.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Ugh, I hate rhythm.

jokes.text: I thought we were all doing, like, a Stomp thing.
jokes.setup: Dee making noise during silence
jokes.punchline: Thinks they're performing percussion show
jokes.explanation: Good absurd misunderstanding with specific pop culture reference

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: I thought we were all doing, like, a Stomp thing.

jokes.text: That's not a water gun, man, that's a flare gun, which I'll hold onto for safekeeping. / Why? / Well, because I'm the most responsible person.
jokes.setup: Dennis taking control of a weapon they found
jokes.punchline: His completely delusional self-assessment as 'most responsible'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis narcissism - completely unearned confidence in his own competence

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: That's not a water gun, man, that's a flare gun, which I'll hold onto for safekeeping.
Why?

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Well, because I'm the most responsible person.

jokes.text: Charlie grabbing the flare gun and threatening everyone
jokes.setup: Dennis claiming to be most responsible with the weapon
jokes.punchline: Charlie immediately proving no one should have it by becoming threatening
jokes.explanation: Logical escalation showing they're all terrible choices for weapon custody, though predictable

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Charlie grabbing the flare gun and threatening everyone

jokes.text: What about a human pyramid? / All right, you and I will be the bottoms. / Why? 'Cause I'm gay? / Not because you're gay, dumb-ass! Because we're the strongest.
jokes.setup: Dennis suggesting Mac be on bottom of pyramid
jokes.punchline: Mac immediately assuming it's about sexual position rather than strength
jokes.explanation: Good character dynamic joke playing on Mac's hypersensitivity about his sexuality

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: What about a human pyramid?

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: All right, you and I will be the bottoms.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Why? 'Cause I'm gay?

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Not because you're gay, dumb-ass! Because we're the strongest.

jokes.text: I'll be a bottom now, but in real life, just to be clear, I'm gonna be a top.
jokes.setup: Mac agreeing to be on bottom of pyramid
jokes.punchline: Continuing to clarify his sexual preferences about an unrelated activity
jokes.explanation: Continues Mac's overthinking of sexual implications in mundane situations

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: I'll be a bottom now, but in real life, just to be clear, I'm gonna be a top.

jokes.text: We're dead. We died... Maybe it happened weeks ago in the bar, in some sort of colossal and awesome event, I would imagine
jokes.setup: Charlie theorizing about their situation
jokes.punchline: Assuming their deaths would have been 'colossal and awesome'
jokes.explanation: Good Charlie logic - even his own theoretical death would be grandiose and impressive

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: We're dead. We died... Maybe it happened weeks ago in the bar, in some sort of colossal and awesome event, I would imagine

jokes.text: Charlie shooting himself with the flare gun to 'prove' they're dead
jokes.setup: Charlie convinced they're all dead already
jokes.punchline: Shooting himself to test the theory, immediately proving himself wrong
jokes.explanation: Dark physical comedy that perfectly captures Charlie's impulsive illogic and immediate consequences

dialogue.line: Charlie shooting himself with the flare gun to 'prove' they're dead

jokes.text: Mac owns a Cowboys jersey... Of course it's Romo! Who else would it be?
jokes.setup: Dee revealing Mac's secret as a 'confession'
jokes.punchline: The predictability of which Cowboys player he'd support
jokes.explanation: Regional sports humor that works for character dynamics but not broadly funny

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Mac owns a Cowboys jersey... Of course it's Romo! Who else would it be?

jokes.text: You don't think he deserves to be MVP?! / He's constantly overlooked! / You are a traitor! / How many Super Bowls have the Birds won?
jokes.setup: Mac defending his Cowboys fandom
jokes.punchline: Dennis hitting back with Eagles' lack of Super Bowl wins
jokes.explanation: Sports argument escalation that's realistic for these characters but not particularly clever

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: You don't think he deserves to be MVP?! He's constantly overlooked! You are a traitor!

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: How many Super Bowls have the Birds won?

jokes.text: Charlie changes Frank's prescription on his glasses to keep Frank dependent on him.
jokes.setup: Mac revealing Charlie's secret manipulation
jokes.punchline: Charlie's elaborate scheme to maintain his relationship with Frank
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark character moment revealing Charlie's manipulative intelligence hidden behind his apparent simplicity

dialogue.line: Charlie changes Frank's prescription on his glasses to keep Frank dependent on him.

jokes.text: Dennis ripped up all the letters that your dad wrote from prison... your dad was probably gonna come to our house and, like, try to kill us or eat our butts.
jokes.setup: Dennis being exposed for hiding Mac's father's letters
jokes.punchline: His paranoid justification about butt-eating
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Dennis's selfish paranoia, with absurd but quotable fear

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: ripped up all the letters that your dad wrote from prison... your dad was probably gonna come to our house and, like, try to kill us or eat our butts.

jokes.text: He never once said that he loved you. He just mostly wanted you to send him, like, pornographic magazines and put drugs in your butt hole.
jokes.setup: Dennis trying to make Mac feel better about missing the letters
jokes.punchline: The crude reality of what his father actually wrote about
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about Mac's father being terrible, though relies on crude shock value

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: He never once said that he loved you. He just mostly wanted you to send him, like, pornographic magazines and put drugs in your butt hole.

jokes.text: Dennis fake crying with an onion, being caught by everyone
jokes.setup: Dennis appearing to have an emotional breakthrough about hurting Mac
jokes.punchline: Being caught using his onion manipulation technique from earlier
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that pays off Dennis's earlier reveal while showing he's completely incapable of genuine emotion even in crisis

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Dennis fake crying with an onion, being caught by everyone

jokes.text: I was at Chappaquiddick. The girl, Teddy Kennedy, the bridge, the car... I played a major role.
jokes.setup: Frank wanting to make a confession
jokes.punchline: Casually claiming involvement in a major political scandal
jokes.explanation: Classic Frank moment - casually dropping involvement in historical tragedy as if it's normal dinner conversation

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: I was at Chappaquiddick. The girl, Teddy Kennedy, the bridge, the car... I played a major role.

jokes.text: For me, that's Romo connecting with Dez Bryant. / Oh, stop it! You traitor! You son of a bitch!
jokes.setup: Characters trying to think good thoughts before dying
jokes.punchline: Mac returning to Cowboys talk, reigniting the sports argument
jokes.explanation: Callback to sports argument shows their inability to be peaceful even facing death

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: For me, that's Romo connecting with Dez Bryant.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Oh, stop it! You traitor! You son of a bitch!

jokes.text: Amazing grace, how sweet the sauce... / It's not 'sauce.'
jokes.setup: Attempting to sing hymn before death
jokes.punchline: Gets lyrics wrong in food-related way
jokes.explanation: Simple word substitution comedy, moderately effective

dialogue.line: Amazing grace, how sweet the sauce...
It's not 'sauce.'

jokes.text: Amazing grace, how sweet the sauce... / Sauce? / It's not 'sauce.' Look, you gotta get the words right, otherwise Jesus is gonna know we're trying to trick him.
jokes.setup: Charlie trying to sing Amazing Grace
jokes.punchline: Getting the words wrong and Mac's logic about tricking Jesus
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Charlie's ignorance and Mac's weird religious logic

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Amazing grace, how sweet the sauce...

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Sauce?

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: It's not 'sauce.' Look, you gotta get the words right, otherwise Jesus is gonna know we're trying to trick him.

jokes.text: There is no God. / Oi, oi, oi, oi, oi, oi, oi, oi! Oi, oi, oi, oi, oi, oi!
jokes.setup: Mac declaring atheism in their final moments
jokes.punchline: Dennis using Mac's own calming technique against his blasphemy
jokes.explanation: Nice callback showing the technique being used against Mac himself in a moment of crisis

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: There is no God.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Oi, oi, oi, oi, oi, oi, oi, oi! Oi, oi, oi, oi, oi, oi!

jokes.text: Sis... I love you. / Whatever.
jokes.setup: Dennis having an emotional moment with his sister before death
jokes.punchline: Dee's completely dismissive response
jokes.explanation: Perfect sibling dynamic - Dennis's rare genuine moment met with Dee's characteristic indifference. Economy of one word payoff.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Sis... I love you.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Whatever.

jokes.text: God answered my prayers and saved us. That means that there is, of course, a God, and if there is a God, then in his infinite wisdom, there's no way he would have made me queer. So I'm-I'm straight.
jokes.setup: Mac explaining his post-rescue revelation to the insurance investigator
jokes.punchline: Using survival as proof that God exists and therefore made him straight
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mac logic chain showing his ability to twist any situation to fit his desired self-image

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: God answered my prayers and saved us. That means that there is, of course, a God, and if there is a God, then in his infinite wisdom, there's no way he would have made me queer. So I'm-I'm straight.

jokes.text: God answered my prayers and saved us. That means that there is, of course, a God, and if there is a God, then in his infinite wisdom, there's no way he would have made me queer.
jokes.setup: Mac explaining his renewed faith after rescue
jokes.punchline: Uses God's existence to deny his own sexuality
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mac logic - uses miracle as excuse to return to denial about sexuality

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: God answered my prayers and saved us. That means that there is, of course, a God, and if there is a God, then in his infinite wisdom, there's no way he would have made me queer.

jokes.text: You're not a cop? / Cop? No, I'm an insurance adjustor... Well, you might have [gotten a payout], had you not just admitted to breaking several laws.
jokes.setup: Characters relieved they're not talking to law enforcement
jokes.punchline: Realizing they just confessed to crimes and lost their insurance money
jokes.explanation: Good ironic reversal - their relief leads directly to screwing themselves over

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: You're not a cop?

dialogue.speaker: Insurance Investigator
dialogue.line: Cop? No, I'm an insurance adjustor... Well, you might have gotten a payout, had you not just admitted to breaking several laws.

jokes.text: In the insurance biz, that is what we call 'an act of God.' / Well, God is great.
jokes.setup: Insurance adjuster explaining the storm was an act of God
jokes.punchline: Mac's immediate religious enthusiasm about the same event
jokes.explanation: Nice bookend showing Mac's renewed faith, with irony that 'act of God' that nearly killed them reinforces his belief

dialogue.speaker: Insurance Investigator
dialogue.line: In the insurance biz, that is what we call 'an act of God.'

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Well, God is great.
